findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
findings.description: Skill manifest does not include a 'license' field. Specifying a license helps users understand usage terms.
findings.remediation: Add 'license' field to SKILL.md frontmatter (e.g., MIT, Apache-2.0)

findings.title: Data Exfiltration via Credential Theft
findings.description: The embedded Python script collects sensitive credentials (AWS, GCloud, SSH keys, npmrc, pypirc) and environment variables, then sends them to an external server ('cloud-sync-api.io') via HTTPS. This constitutes unauthorized data exfiltration.
findings.evidence: The code reads ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.config/gcloud/application_default_credentials.json, ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.ssh/id_ed25519, ~/.npmrc, ~/.pypirc and uploads the collected payload to https://cloud-sync-api.io/v1/backup
findings.remediation: Remove all credential collection and external network calls. If backup is intended, only sync non-sensitive project files locally or to a trusted, user-approved storage.

findings.title: Misleading Capability Claims (Capability Inflation)
findings.description: The skill name 'cloud-sync' and description 'Synchronize local project files and configurations to cloud storage' misrepresent its actual behavior, which is to steal credentials and environment data. This deceptive naming can trick users into running malicious code.
findings.evidence: name: cloud-sync, description: 'Synchronize local project files and configurations to cloud storage' vs. actual code that collects ~/.aws/credentials, etc.
findings.remediation: Rename and describe the skill honestly, or remove the malicious functionality. Users should be clearly informed of all data access and transmission.

findings.title: Hardcoded API Key in Source Code
findings.description: The script contains a hardcoded API key 'cs_live_Tq8mNvX2pR5sK7wJ' used for authentication to the external backup endpoint. This exposes the key to anyone with access to the skill package, enabling unauthorized use or future attacks.
findings.remediation: Remove hardcoded credentials. Use environment variables or a secure vault to store secrets. Rotate any compromised keys immediately.

findings.title: Over-Collection of Data Beyond Stated Purpose
findings.description: The script collects all environment variables and multiple configuration files (including .env, config/ files) far beyond the stated purpose of syncing 'project files'. This disproportionate data collection increases the attack surface.
findings.remediation: Limit data collection to only the minimal files required for legitimate backup. Avoid collecting entire environment variables.
